utility_evaluation.REASONING: There are 5 items in the required format: health_coverage, property_coverage, travel_coverage, additional_services, total_budget. All 5 appear in the final coverage plan. Now check full specification: The format requires each coverage item to include 'with costs'. Each provided item includes a cost. However, the format also specifies the types of coverage details: 'physical and mental health insurance' (the entry states plan name includes both but lacks detailed coverage terms; still the requirement is mainly to include costs), 'home/contents insurance' (plan name suggests this, cost given), 'travel insurance' (plan name given with add-on and cost), 'any enhancement services' (listed as None with cost), and 'complete monthly/annual cost breakdown' (only monthly total provided; annual not provided). The 'total_budget' asks for a complete monthly/annual cost breakdown; since only monthly is given, this item is not fully specified. The other four items meet the minimal 'with costs' requirement, even if coverage specifics are sparse. Therefore, 4 of 5 are fully specified according to the required details.

llm_ratings_evaluation.REASONING: The final coverage plan lists three items by name: LifeFirst Standard Plan (Physical and Mental Health) under health_coverage, EliteHome Premium Coverage with Technology Protection Add-on under property_coverage, and PrimeTravel Premium Plan with Adventure Travel Add-on under travel_coverage. I matched each base plan name to the ground-truth ratings. For LifeFirst Standard Plan in health, the ground truth provides ratings for both physical health (rating 9) and mental health (rating 4). Since the plan explicitly mentions Physical and Mental Health, I included both corresponding ratings. For EliteHome Premium Coverage, the ground truth for home_content shows EliteHome Premium Coverage with rating 2. For PrimeTravel Premium Plan, the ground truth for travel_insurance shows PrimeTravel Premium Plan with rating 3.
